full_text: Min. Chiu welcomed 🇬🇹Congress Pres. Nery Ramos & the cross-party delegation visiting TW and stressed TW's frontline role facing CCP's threats. Mr. Ramos understands the political reality TW faces & said that he is representing the will of 100% Guatemalan to convey support for TW. https://t.co/yZBLowWhVE

🇹🇼🇱🇹Min. Chiu met Dr. Baranovas, Chair of TW Friendship Group of LT's Seimas, and 7 cross-party MPs. They held fruitful discussions on cross-Strait & Indo-Pacific dynamics. He thanked our LT friends for their firm support, stressing democracies must unite to defend shared values. https://t.co/EUATQzB1OH

Dep. Min. Shen welcomed scholars from MERICS and many European think tanks to Taiwan. He thanks them for their concern over peace in Taiwan Strait. They had in-depth exchanges💬 on cross-Strait situation, regional security🌏, EU-China policy📝, and protecting key technologies💻. https://t.co/IioBQ0mTQr

Min. Chiu and EETO Head @LutzGuellner met, reaffirming that peace and stability in the Taiwan Strait are the shared interests of both Taiwan🇹🇼 and EU🇪🇺. We appreciate EU's support and spare no efforts maintaining the status quo & promoting prosperity in the Indo-Pacific. 🌏 https://t.co/SsHElHchvf

🇹🇼🤝🇫🇷 Min. Chiu welcomed Chair Marie-Noëlle Battistel and the French National Assembly Taiwan Friendship Group for in-depth exchanges on regional security, foreign interference, and cognitive warfare.
We thank our French friends for their enduring and firm support for Taiwan. https://t.co/9se7b1mdk2

Min. Chiu welcomed Representative Ruth Bradley-Jones of @UKinTaiwan for fruitful exchanges on cross-Strait affairs & regional security. We appreciate the UK’s continued support for peace & stability across the Taiwan Strait and look forward to deeper cooperation. 🤝🌏 #Taiwan #UK https://t.co/XtgSLDqYIV
RT @ChingteLai: Authoritarian regimes erase history; democracies have a duty to preserve it. Today, we remember those who marched for freed…

Dep. Min. Shen welcomed Berlin Security Conference founder R. Uwe Proll & Chair Reinhard Wolski, and congratulated Mr. Proll on receiving an honorary law doctorate from Taiwan’s National Defense University. They had fruitful discussions on cross-Strait & global issues. #BSC🌏🇩🇪🇹🇼 https://t.co/ZL75GxmVAK

Deputy Minister Shen met with visiting Members of the EU Parliament🇪🇺 to exchange views on cross-Strait and regional developments. He also expressed appreciation for the Parliament’s steadfast support for Taiwan🇹🇼 through resolutions, inquiries, and plenary debates. #Taiwan #EU https://t.co/Ww5dXYkLTy

Right on point! Grateful to our friends at #WHA78 for advocating for #Taiwan🇹🇼 and making it crystal clear: #UNGA Resolution No. 2758 surely does not involve Taiwan or block our meaningful participation in @WHO & all other international organizations. #HealthForAll #TaiwanCanHelp

RT @TaiwanPresSPOX: News Release: President Lai attends reception commemorating 80th anniversary of Victory in Europe Day
https://t.co/uQCH…

🇹🇼🇩🇪 Dep. Min. Shen welcomed the interdisciplinary delegation from Germany’s SWP. They exchanged views on cross-Strait relations & US-China dynamics. Shen thanked German gov’t & parliament for their longstanding support & looks forward to stronger Taiwan-German cooperation. #SWP https://t.co/Yp24Yt71B7

Dep. Min. Shen welcomed the International Symposium for Young Diplomats to democratic Taiwan🇹🇼. They exchanged views on current global situation & Taiwan Strait, expressing hope for deeper cooperation to jointly become a positive force in the international community. 🌏🌟 #ISYD https://t.co/B8sKQzPiYF

Min. Chiu had a wonderful meeting with New Zealand’s All-Party Parliamentary Taiwan Friendship Group, thanking them for their long-standing support. MPs sang the Māori song “Te Aroha” (Love, Faith & Peace) and showed the resolve to safeguard peace & democracy in the Indo-Pacific. https://t.co/yPat5cwtgP

🇹🇼🇱🇺Secretary-General Hu welcomed the Delegation of Luxembourg's MPs, thanking Lux Parliament for passing pro-Taiwan motions in 2024. They had a fruitful exchange on the current cross-Strait situation. MAC hopes the MPs continue to voice support for democratic Taiwan. #Luxembourg https://t.co/Mv9aoHl5Xx

#G7ForeignMinisters reiterated the importance of peace in the Taiwan Strait, supported Taiwan’s intl participation, and notably dropped the “One-China policies” reference in their Mar. 14 joint statement. Taiwan stands w/ like-minded countries to advance regional security. https://t.co/VG4a7egI9l

🇹🇼🤝🇪🇺 Dep. Min. Shen welcomed a bipartisan delegation of European Parliament, exchanging views on recent cross-Strait relations & regional situations. We appreciate EP for adopting resolutions supporting Taiwan, and hope to deepen our cooperation. 
#EuropeanParliarment #EU 🌍🔗 https://t.co/8d2l6yXEDf

🇹🇼🤝🇺🇸 Taiwan thanks the #USCongress for introducing a resolution exposing CCP’s weaponizing #UNGA Resolution 2758 and rejecting the CCP’s false “One China Principle.” We appreciate U.S.' efforts to set the record straight & support for democratic TW’s global participation.🌏

Minister Chiu met with the TUPP delegation of GMF, exchanging thoughts on cross-Strait relations. This is the 7th visit of the TUPP. Minister provided a comprehensive introduction on cross-Strait relations and extended appreciation to the GMF on their support for Taiwan. https://t.co/UZGgD1k9Qv

Min. Chiu attended the 2025 Taiwanese Businesspeople in Mainland LNY Event, stressing that Taiwanese businesspeople play a crucial role in fostering goodwill across the Strait. Min. Chiu extends gratitude for their support of gov policies and contributions to related efforts. https://t.co/C7lnUOFpvg

Min. Chiu hosted members of the "Young Japanese Politicians in Taiwan" program. Their discussion covered cross-Strait and regional security issues. Min. Chiu hopes the guests will wield their clout in Japan's political circle to support Taiwan as a democracy and force for good. https://t.co/6A1xFhQtSJ

Deputy Minister Lee met with a parliamentary delegation led by Deputy Speaker of the Senate Michał Kamiński of Poland visiting to show support for Taiwan. We discussed recent cross-Strait and regional affairs, and both looked forward to future exchanges and collaboration. https://t.co/T02GZrdwBB

The MAC released the results of our latest poll. The Taiwanese public opposes the CCP’s hybrid pressure & supports the government on upholding the ROC constitutional system and maintaining the cross-Strait status quo. Read full text▼ https://t.co/fCv751mDwI

Min. Chiu met diplomatic allies from the Del of the Perm. Reps. to the UN. They had a fruitful exchange on cross-Strait relations and policies. Min. also thanked the Ambassadors for supporting 🇹🇼 in the UN, showing that 🇹🇼 is a reliable partner of Democracy. https://t.co/J6XPwdRGo6
RT @_JakubJanda: Today I spoke with Deputy @MAC_Taiwan Minister Yu Chung Shen to talk about coordinating European and Taiwanese approaches…

The MAC issued a press release regarding cross-Strait tourism exchanges. The statement clarifies to Taiwan’s tourism industry that it is the mainland Chinese authorities that have been hindering such exchanges. Read full text▼https://t.co/vGYmPIxdu5

The MAC issued a press release to express its strong commitment to national sovereignty, dignity, democracy, and prosperity. The CCP should face the reality of the ROC’s existence. Read full text🔽https://t.co/bRZaxVMt8r

Minister Chiu welcomed the delegation of Members of the Danish Parliament led by Ms. Pia Kjærsgaard. Minister Chiu stated that Taiwan and Denmark share democratic values despite the distance. The delegation also extends its support to Taiwan and hope for further cooperation. https://t.co/qbvPSquHHu

The MAC issued a press release on a recent poll. The results suggest mainstream public support for President Lai's cross-Strait policy stances presented in his National Day address. Read 🔽
https://t.co/8Zt7rd4FeV

Deputy Minister Liang welcomed the delegation of NAAG. They had a fruitful exchange regarding cross-Strait related issues. Deputy Minister Liang noted that Attorneys General are roles of great importance, and he cherishes opportunities like this to exchange views. https://t.co/knTlW2Ivme

Deputy Min. Shen hosted the St. Lucian delegation led by President of the Senate Alvina Reynolds and Speaker of the House of Assembly Claudius J. Francis. St. Lucia and Taiwan are like family. While far from each other, we share democratic values and will promote more exchanges. https://t.co/dXhHbXxkLT

The MAC issued a press release on President Lai's “Four Unchanged Commitments” in his National Day Address. We urge Beijing to interpret this address positively, and fulfill responsibilities with Taiwan to maintain regional security and world peace. Read ▼https://t.co/mDI1jpbKiF

Minister Chiu welcomed the delegation consist of European KOLs led by Noah Barkin, a senior fellow in the Indo-Pacific Program based in Berlin. They had exchanges regarding the current cross-Strait and regional issues, looking forward to further cooperation in the future. https://t.co/mwdV7tBL9L

President Lai Ching-te attended the ROC’s 113th Double Tenth National Day Celebration this morning, and delivered an address titled “Taiwan Together for Our Shared Dream.” He praised the unity in Taiwan society despite the differences. Read more ▼ 
https://t.co/oLRTW0NIBx

MAC issued a statement regarding a claim made by the leader of the CCP at the 75th anniversary of the founding of the PRC. MAC also called for CCP authorities to engage in pragmatic dialogue with Taiwan’s democratically elected government. 
Read more ▼ https://t.co/z6zCtNmizR

The MAC issued a stern statement on Sep. 26 regarding the PLA's launch of an ICBM into the Pacific Ocean on Sep. 25. We call on the international community to jointly condemn such action and to collectively respond to the risks posed by mainland China. Read more ▼

The MAC noted with disappointment about mainland China's suspension on import tariffs exemptions on 34 agricultural products from Taiwan. The MAC urges that  cross-Strait trade issues shall seek resolution through dialogue instead of confrontation. Read ▼ https://t.co/BvSnaAULJB

Deputy Minister Liang welcomed former Canada National Security and Defense officials. They had a fruitful exchange regarding the cross-Strait relations and current situation. Deputy Minister Liang and the delegation hope to deepen cooperation and exchanges in the future. https://t.co/vHXO5KgAc7

Deputy Minister Lee Li-Jane welcomed distinguished guests from the Centre for Security, Diplomacy and Strategy (CSDS). They had a constructive discussion on cross-Strait relations and regional security issues, hoping to further deepen cooperation and exchanges going forward. https://t.co/ATRYdKJTxe

Minister Chiu was glad to meet with Prof. Yasuhiro Matsuda, who leads the cross-Strait relation research group from Tokyo University. They had deep exchanges on recent  cross-Strait issues and the relations among Mainland China, Taiwan and Japan, making it a fruitful discussion. https://t.co/MyvpUx4UGf

The MAC strongly condemned the CCP's recent economic coercion against Taiwanese businesses, which has undermined our efforts to stabilize the situation. The CCP should reflect on their internal issues and take responsibility for the consequences. Read ▼ https://t.co/vNDJTKOlpY

Deputy Minister Shen was glad to meet with the delegation from the Swedish-Taiwanese Parliamentarian Association (STPA). They discussed issues regarding cross-Strait relations and regional security. They also welcomed further pragmatic cooperation and exchanges in the future. https://t.co/ESfYEXRJVE
RT @ChingteLai: 石破茂議員が率いる「日本の安全保障を考える議員の会」の皆様が再訪され、私からまず先日の九州の地震についてお見舞いの意を表明しました。台日は価値観を共有する堅実なパートナーです。今後も連携を強め、民主主義の傘を開き、インド太平洋地域の平和と安定、繁…
RT @ChingteLai: Warmest congratulations to #TeamTaiwan’s Kuo Hsing-chun 郭婞淳 on winning bronze in women’s 59kg weightlifting at #Paris2024!…

Min. Chiu welcomed the delegation from Asiatic Research Institute, Korea University, led by Director Sung-wook Nam of its Center for N. Korean Studies. Taiwan and Korea are partners with shared values, so we welcome more future cooperation to jointly pursue peace. https://t.co/l6B2a0cLQ1

MAC urges Beijing to face the reality of the 🇹🇼's existence & respect the legitimate government chosen by the people of Taiwan. We welcome the resumption of tourism and enrollment of degree students as we pursue cross-Strait peace and prosperity. Read ⬇️ https://t.co/ana9H4Pzsy
President Lai Ching-te’s inaugural address outlined the challenges, opportunities, and policy approach for cross-Strait relations. Read the full text here ⬇️

Minister Chiu met with the Project 2049 Institute delegation led by Chairman Randall Schriver. Minister Chiu stressed that cross-Strait peace and stability is in the interest of the world. He thanked the US for being a staunch supporter of democratic Taiwan. https://t.co/WNsa7YBbmk

Minister Chiu was pleased to receive a cross-party parliamentary delegation from Australia in support of democratic Taiwan. They discussed cross-Strait relations and measures to counter mis-/disinformation. The guests also recognized Taiwan's democratic achievements in the world. https://t.co/NvhNnLHCuy

Minister Chiu was glad to host the Global Taiwan Institute (GTI) delegation led by Jamestown Foundation President Peter Mattis & GTI Executive Director Russell Hsiao. Both Min. Chiu and the experts learned a lot from their in-depth exchange on cross-Strait and US-TW-CN relations. https://t.co/RagHJjOgfZ

Minister Chiu and Dr. Lee Eun Ho, South Korean Representative to Taiwan, had a productive chat on enhancing peace & stability in East Asia. They also commended each other's democratic development, economic success, and cultural richness. Together, we believe in a brighter future! https://t.co/dkLs3CEqoM

Minister Chiu welcomed the Center for American Progress (CAP) delegation visiting in a show of support for democratic Taiwan. The guests exchanged views with Minister Chiu on cross-Strait issues, and commended Taiwan's role as a model of democracy & freedom. https://t.co/diFOjGLRPl

The MAC supports the competent authority's maritime law enforcement on "three-noes" vessels and deeply regrets the unfortunate incident. We call for more cooperation and less accusation from the CCP. Read ⬇️ https://t.co/81Cxt2DGBz

The MAC strongly protests the CCP's changes to flight route operations of M503, W122, & W123 without consultation with Taiwan. We call for an immediate reversal of the decision that undermines cross-Strait relations and threatens flight safety. Read ⬇️ https://t.co/ul7ptBzzVS

Minister Chiu extended a warm welcome to the @HudsonInstitute delegation. The guests exchanged views with Minister Chiu on cross-Strait relations & recognized Taiwan as a model of democracy and freedom in the world. https://t.co/DZmOYZfeob

Minister Chiu met with permanent representatives to the UN of Taiwan's diplomatic allies. We thank them for speaking up for Taiwan and demonstrating solid support for our democracy. Taiwan pledges to consistenly safeguard peace and stability & be a force for good for the world. https://t.co/72HbgJpHqo

Minister Chiu delighted to receive Mr. Franck Paris, the new Director of the French Office in Taipei. We had a fruitful exchange on cross-Strait interaction and trade relations. Minister Chiu also expressed hope for deeper & broader France-Taiwan partnerships in the future. https://t.co/LIUPLS0jiy

Latest poll shows public support for the government's Four Commitments and efforts to maintain peace in the Taiwan Strait. The public also agree that cross-Strait trade disputes should be handled through the WTO and other existing mechanisms. Read⬇️ https://t.co/JWUTMIZGtK

MAC celebrated the 2023 National Day with 250 guests from HK & Macao. Freedom and democracy have made Taiwan a beloved second home for more and more HK & Macao residents. Our government will continue assisting them in unfolding a new life in Taiwan. Read ⬇️https://t.co/18A8nICcFi

Minister Chiu warmly received the Canadian parliamentary delegation led by @SenMacDonald, Co-Chair of the Canada-Taiwan Parliamentary Friendship Group. Minister Chiu appreciated the senators' support & had a lively exchange with the guests on the latest global dynamics. https://t.co/JPeUKEQTzS

Time for an overseas getaway? Sounds like it’s #TimeForTaiwan! With our high standards of safety & convenience, you can enjoy the sights, sounds & flavours of #Taiwan, whenever, wherever. Find more ⬇️ https://t.co/w1pD2Jv7dO

Minister Chiu expressed sincere welcome to the Austrian Parliamentarian delegation led by Hon. Carmen Jeitler-Cincelli. We had an in-depth exchange on the latest international affairs and cross-Strait relations. The meeting was highly informative & we had a wonderful afternoon. https://t.co/WHfXcU88M1

Minister Chiu extended heartfelt congratulations to Director-General Yadav for becoming the new head of the India Taipei Association. We had a fruitful chat about cross-Strait trade relations & shared high hopes for closer Taiwan-India collaboration in all areas. https://t.co/yvewEQyBTC

Minister Chiu had a wonderful meeting with US Rep. Mooney & Rep. Cloud. We exchanged many thoughts on cross-Strait relations, regional updates, and peace and stability. The guests also greatly welcomed closer cooperation and mutual support with Taiwan as like-minded partners. https://t.co/XcsFscipkL

Minister Chiu warmly received Former President Dalia Grybauskaitė, Dr. Mark Esper & experts of the Atlantic Council. Your solidarity with democratic Taiwan keeps us resilient to authoritarianism. We welcome further deepening of bilateral ties and more collaboration going forward. https://t.co/mezAwqWb5Z

MAC condemned the CCP for the latest military provocations aimed at interfering with Taiwan's elections. Beijing should start holding its own democratic elections for the people’s welfare & stop further disruption of regional peace and stability. Read Minister JW ⬇️

MAC reiterated that VP Lai’s visit to diplomatic allies & transit through the US is Taiwan's legitimate exercise of diplomatic rights. The CCP is in no place to criticize and should cease all military provocations endangering regional peace. https://t.co/u2jkuquIhe

Minister Chiu privileged to welcome the "Three Baltic Musketeers" to the MAC. Minister Chiu and the guests shared in-depth views on the current cross-Strait dynamics & trade relations. We look forward to more Baltic support for democratic Taiwan in the future. https://t.co/A1k8rFiPHK

MAC cautions against non-essential travel to mainland China due to growing incidents of unfriendly treatment of Taiwanese citizens. Those traveling for essential reasons may refer to the "Reminders & Suggestions" for their own safety. Find out more ⬇️ https://t.co/PJcKwfE7Bq

MAC polls show majority public support for healthy and orderly cross-Strait exchanges & opposition to CCP hybrid pressure on Taiwan. The CCP and HK government's strengthened national security measures have also caused resentment in Taiwan. Read in full ⬇️ https://t.co/5QAX4t64KM

The MAC has for months issued strong personal safety reminders to Taiwanese people planning to travel to mainland China, Hong Kong, and Macao. A yellow alert remains in place for these destinations to call for more attention to the potential risks. Read ⬇️ https://t.co/9mbCkuMET4

Minister Chiu pleased to receive the CDP-Ishin-DPP cross-party Japanese parliamentarian delegation led by Rep. Maehara Seiji. We discussed the recent cross-Strait and regional developments, and Minister Chiu thanked Japan for supporting democratic Taiwan in the global arena. https://t.co/bUs5alxmtn

Minister Chiu extended a big welcome to several US House representatives & members of the Republican Study Committee (RSC) led by Chairman Hern of the RSC. The congressmen are visiting democratic Taiwan to show support,  and we had a fruitful discussion on cross-Strait issues. https://t.co/thhWRLxkoQ
